title: Rebasing vs. Merging Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Git branch history management concepts.

questions.question: Which of these is a key difference between merging and rebasing?
options: Merging is only used in SQL; Rebasing is for NoSQL
Merging preserves the original branch history; Rebasing rewrites it for a linear timeline
Merging is faster than rebasing by 10x

questions.question: Regarding Git branching, which technique 'replays' your branch's individual commits on top of another branch's latest commit?
What is a potential risk of using 'rebase' instead of 'merge'?
options: Rewriting history that has already been pushed to a shared remote repository
It deletes all your local work by mistake
